title: Harden a C build with warnings, sanitizers, and static analysis
description: Layer strict warning flags, runtime sanitizers, and a static analyzer to catch memory and integer bugs the standard does not require diagnosing.
body: Steps: 1. Build with -Wall -Wextra -Wconversion -Werror and treat warnings as defects. 2. Add a sanitized build target (-fsanitize=address,undefined -g) and run the test suite under it. 3. Run a static analyzer (clang --analyze / clang-tidy / cppcheck) over the sources and triage findings. 4. For untrusted input paths, fuzz with libFuzzer/AFL. 5. Record which findings are real vs suppressed-with-justification. Rollback point: before suppressing any analyzer warning. Validation: the sanitized suite passes clean and the analyzer reports no new high-severity issues. Risk boundary: a suppressed warning on a memory or integer-overflow path can be an exploitable bug; require an explicit justification comment and human review rather than a blanket suppression.
